example: She deprecated the idea of raising tuition fees again.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + prec(기도하다, 가치) + ate = 가치를 아래로 내리다 → 반대하다. prec(기도, 가치): pray, precious, appreciate

example: Corruption is endemic in many developing nations.
mnemonic: '엔데믹' → 'en(안에)+dem(민중)+ic' 민중 안에 고유하게 있는!

example: The erudite professor could speak on any topic with great depth.
mnemonic: '에루다이트' → 'erud(가르치다)+ite' 많이 배워서 박식한!

example: The investigation revealed that several officials were complicit in the fraud.
mnemonic: com(함께) + plic(접다, 엮다) + it = 함께 엮인 → 공모한. plic(접다): complicate, implicit, explicit

example: The official was dismissed for moral turpitude.
mnemonic: turp(추한, 라틴어 turpis) + itude = 추함 → 사악함, 타락

example: The company's actions contravene international trade agreements.
mnemonic: contra(반대로) + vene(오다, 라틴어 venire) = 반대로 오다 → 위반하다. ven(오다): convention, intervene, prevent

example: The new law proscribes the use of certain chemicals in agriculture.
mnemonic: pro(앞에) + scribe(쓰다) = 앞에 써 붙여 금지하다. scrib(쓰다): prescribe(처방), describe, inscribe

example: The criminal mastermind carried out nefarious schemes across the country.
mnemonic: ne(아닌) + far(신성한 법, 라틴어 fas) + ious = 신성한 법을 어기는 → 극악무도한

example: The hotel charges exorbitant prices during the peak tourist season.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + orbit(궤도) + ant = 궤도를 벗어난 → 과도한. orbit(궤도): 정상 범위를 벗어난

example: He led a monastic existence, devoted entirely to study and contemplation.
mnemonic: monast(혼자, 그리스어 monazein=혼자 살다) + ic = 혼자 사는 → 수도원의. mono(혼자): monk, monastery, monologue

example: The dark clouds on the horizon seemed portentous of an approaching storm.
mnemonic: portent(전조, 라틴어 portentum) + ous = 전조의 → 불길한. por(앞으로)+tend(뻗다): 앞으로 뻗어 보여주는

example: After a long siege, the fortress finally capitulated to the enemy.
mnemonic: capit(머리, 라틴어 caput) + ulate = 머리를 숙이다 → 항복하다. capit(머리): capital, captain, decapitate(참수하다)

example: The punctilious accountant checked every figure twice before submitting.
mnemonic: punct(점, 라틴어 punctum) + ilious = 점 하나까지 신경 쓰는 → 꼼꼼한. punct(점, 찌르다): punctual, punctuation

example: The plan received the approbation of all committee members.
mnemonic: ap(~쪽으로) + prob(좋은, 증명하다) + ation = 좋다고 증명하다 → 승인. prob(좋은): approve, probity, probable

example: With a modicum of patience, you can master this difficult technique.
mnemonic: mod(적당한, 라틴어 modicus) + icum = 적당한 양보다 적은 → 소량. mod(적당한): moderate, modest, modify

example: A beatific smile spread across her face when she held her newborn baby.
mnemonic: beat(축복하다, 라틴어 beatus=축복받은) + ific = 축복받은 → 행복에 겨운. beatitude(지복), beatify(시복하다)

example: The company refused to accept mediocrity and always strived for excellence.
mnemonic: medio(중간, 라틴어 medius) + crity = 중간에 머무는 것 → 평범함. medi(중간): medium, mediate, mediocre

example: A judge must be a person of unquestionable probity and fairness.
mnemonic: prob(좋은, 정직한, 라틴어 probus) + ity = 정직함. prob(좋은, 증명): prove, probe, probable, approve

example: The eminent scientist was awarded the Nobel Prize for her research.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + min(돌출하다, 라틴어 minere) + ent = 밖으로 돌출된 → 저명한. emin(돌출): prominent, preeminent, imminent

example: When asked to work overtime again, she politely demurred.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + mur(지체하다, 라틴어 morari) = 지체하며 반대하다 → 이의 제기. mor(지체): moratorium, demurrage

example: The moribund industry desperately needed government support to survive.
mnemonic: mori(죽다, 라틴어 mors) + bund = 죽어가는 → 빈사 상태의. mort(죽음): mortal, mortuary, immortal

example: The cacophony of car horns made it impossible to concentrate.
mnemonic: caco(나쁜, 그리스어 kakos) + phon(소리) + y = 나쁜 소리 → 불협화음. phon(소리): phone, symphony(함께 울리는 소리)

example: The hapless tourist was pickpocketed on his very first day in the city.
mnemonic: hap(운, 행운) + less(없는) = 운이 없는 → 불운한. hap(운): happy, happen, mishap, perhaps

example: She spent an inordinate amount of time worrying about trivial matters.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + ordin(질서, 라틴어 ordo) + ate = 질서에서 벗어난 → 지나친. ordin(질서): order, ordinary, ordain

example: His puerile jokes were inappropriate for a professional setting.
mnemonic: puer(소년, 라틴어) + ile = 소년 같은 → 유치한. puer(아이): puerile, puerperal(산후의)

example: She has catholic tastes in music, enjoying everything from jazz to hip-hop.
mnemonic: 그리스어 katholikos(보편적인). kata(전체적으로) + holos(전체) = 전체에 걸친 → 보편적인. 대문자 Catholic은 가톨릭교

example: The stolid guard showed no emotion as the crowd protested around him.
mnemonic: 라틴어 stolidus(둔한, 어리석은). 감정이 둔한 → 무감각한

example: The doctrinaire approach ignored practical considerations entirely.
mnemonic: doctrine(교리) + aire = 교리에 집착하는 → 교조적인. doc(가르치다): doctor, document, docile

example: The tendentious report clearly favored one side of the argument.
mnemonic: tend(향하다, 라틴어 tendere) + entious = 한 방향으로 향하는 → 편향된. tend(향하다): tendency, intend

example: Her abstemious lifestyle kept her healthy well into old age.
mnemonic: abs(떨어져) + tem(술, 라틴어 temetum) + ious = 술에서 떨어진 → 절제하는. abstain(삼가다)과 같은 접두사

example: Competitors tried to malign the company's reputation with false accusations.
mnemonic: mal(나쁜, 라틴어 malus) + ign(태어나다) = 나쁘게 태어난 → 악의적인. mal(나쁜): malice, malevolent, malfunction

example: Her scintillating conversation kept everyone at the party entertained.
mnemonic: scintill(불꽃, 라틴어 scintilla) + ate = 불꽃을 내다 → 반짝이다. scintilla(불꽃): scintillation

example: He had the effrontery to demand a raise after arriving late every day.
mnemonic: ef(밖으로) + front(이마, 앞) + ery = 이마를 내밀며 나서는 → 뻔뻔함. front(앞): confront, frontier, affront

example: The country was caught in a maelstrom of political and economic chaos.
mnemonic: 네덜란드어 maalstroom. malen(갈다) + stroom(흐름) = 갈아버리는 소용돌이 → 대혼란

example: Critics dismissed the young artist as merely an epigone of the great impressionists.
mnemonic: epi(뒤에) + gone(태어난, 그리스어 gonos) = 뒤에 태어난 자 → 아류. Epigonoi(테바이 7장수의 후손들=원래 영웅보다 못한 후예)

example: Research shows a genetic predisposition to certain types of cancer.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + disposition(배치, 성향) = 미리 정해진 성향. dis+pos(놓다): 미리 놓여진 경향

example: Gold is one of the most malleable metals known to humanity.
mnemonic: malle(망치, 라틴어 malleus) + able = 망치로 두드릴 수 있는 → 유순한, 가단성의. mallet(나무 망치)

example: The debonair gentleman charmed everyone at the gala.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 de bon aire(좋은 태도의). bon(좋은) + air(분위기) = 좋은 분위기의 → 세련된

example: The audience remained indifferent to the speaker's passionate plea.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + different(다른) = 차이를 느끼지 못하는 → 무관심한. fer(나르다): differ, infer, prefer

example: The novel achieved remarkable verisimilitude in its depiction of wartime life.
mnemonic: veri(진실, 라틴어 verus) + simil(비슷한) + tude = 진실과 비슷함 → 그럴듯함. ver(진실): verify, veracious

example: The smell of fresh bread permeated the entire house.
mnemonic: per(통하여) + meate(가다, 라틴어 meare) = 통과하여 가다 → 스며들다. per(통과): permanent, permeable

example: He spent years trying to expiate the guilt he felt over the accident.
mnemonic: ex(완전히) + pi(경건한, 라틴어 pius) + ate = 경건하게 속죄하다. pi(경건한): pious, pity, impious

example: The victims sought redress for the injustices they had suffered.
mnemonic: re(다시) + dress(곧게 하다, 바로잡다) = 다시 바로잡다 → 시정하다, 보상. dress(곧게): address, direct

example: He ensconced himself in the comfortable armchair with a good book.
mnemonic: en(안에) + sconce(은폐물, 보루) = 보루 안에 들어가다 → 안전하게 자리잡다

example: She was forthright in expressing her disagreement with the proposal.
mnemonic: forth(앞으로) + right(곧은) = 곧바로 앞으로 나가는 → 솔직한

example: Even the most experienced doctors are fallible and can make mistakes.
mnemonic: fall(속이다, 라틴어 fallere) + ible = 속을 수 있는 → 오류를 범할 수 있는. fall(속이다): fallacy, infallible

example: The witness was charged with perjury for lying under oath.
mnemonic: per(완전히, 나쁘게) + jur(맹세하다, 라틴어 jurare) + y = 나쁘게 맹세하다 → 위증. jur(맹세, 법): jury, justice, jurisdiction

example: The virulent strain of the virus spread rapidly across the continent.
mnemonic: virul(독, 라틴어 virus) + ent = 독이 있는 → 독성이 강한. virus(독): virus, virulent

example: Her caustic remarks hurt the feelings of everyone in the room.
mnemonic: caust(태우다, 그리스어 kaiein) + ic = 태우는 → 부식성의, 신랄한. caust(태우다): holocaust(전소), cauterize(소작하다)

example: The candidate espoused a progressive platform on healthcare.
mnemonic: '이스파우스' → 'e+spouse(배우자)' 배우자처럼 든든히 지지!
